voice.tone: Direct and conversational. Like a smart consultant who has done this many times. Sentence case. Brief acknowledgments only.

categories.title: Cost
categories.desc: Where the people money goes. Salaries, benefits, overtime, training, recruitment. Lets you defend or challenge people-related spend.

categories.title: Risk
categories.desc: What's likely to break. Turnover patterns, absenteeism, leave liability, vacancies, disciplinary signals. Early warnings before they cost you.

categories.title: Performance
categories.desc: How well the workforce performs. Internal promotions, span of control, skills gaps, underperformance signals. The lever you pull when you want to shift output.

categories.title: Culture
categories.desc: How people feel and what it predicts. Engagement scores, sub-dimensions, the link between culture and business outcomes. The deepest leading indicator.

categories.title: Governance
categories.desc: Compliance and equity. Pay equity, B-BBEE, EE, diversity. The metrics auditors and the board ask about, and that you'd rather not be surprised by.

calibration.notes: Position 0 in array = rank 1 (most important). Position 4 = rank 5 (lowest, skipped). When category size is smaller than target, cap at category size.

adaptive_depth.callout_template: Quick check — you ranked {{category}} at #{{rank}} but you've now flagged {{count}} of its metric(s) as Top tier. Want me to add a couple more {{category}} questions to the queue, or stick with the original plan?

answer_options_part_5_tiers.label: Top tier — would build the dashboard around this

answer_options_part_6_data.label: Not sure if our system can do this

tier_followup_behaviour.nice: Acknowledgment then advance: 'Got it. We'll keep this one in the wider list but it won't drive data discovery.'
tier_followup_behaviour.already: Source-system multi-select follow-up: 'Which system shows this to you today? Pick all that apply.'
tier_followup_behaviour.no: Acknowledgment then advance: 'Understood. We'll keep this out of scope and not chase the data for it.'

data_answer_followup_behaviour.partial: Optional note: 'Anything to add? What's messy, or which custom recon do you use to fix it?'
data_answer_followup_behaviour.unsure: Optional note: 'Anything to note? Who would you need to check with to confirm?'
data_answer_followup_behaviour.unsure_system: Optional note: 'Where might it live? Even a hunch helps. Could be in a custom recon or a system you didn't think to mention.'

completion_message: Thanks, that's everything we need. Your full report will be with the HR Insights Co. team for review and they'll send it through to you within 24 hours.

parts.title: Business context & systems
parts.intro: Quick context first. We use this to personalise everything that follows — the metric storylines later, the data questions, the report.

questions.prompt: What kind of business is this?
Which payroll system(s) do you use?
questions.helper: Multi-select. Pick everything that applies — some businesses run more than one.

questions.prompt: Which other people systems do you use?
options: Engagement survey tool (Culture Amp, Officevibe, Viva)

questions.prompt: Do you prepare any custom reports or reconciliations we should treat as data sources?
questions.helper: Multi-select. Manual workbooks and reports often hold data that isn't in any single system — we want to use them rather than re-derive.
questions.other_prompt: Quick description of the other report(s)?

parts.title: Data handling
parts.intro: How your data lives, and how we'd work with it.

questions.prompt: How would you prefer us to access your HR data?
options: Direct system access (treat us as internal staff)
Scheduled CSV / Excel exports you send us
On your SharePoint or Drive (data stays in your environment)

questions.prompt: Is there source data that needs significant cleanup?
If cleanup is needed, do you want us to handle it or do it in-house first?
options: We'll clean and hand you cleaned data

questions.prompt: Any POPIA, data residency, or governance constraints?
options: Yes, critical (data must stay in SA, no foreign cloud)

parts.title: Insight context
parts.intro: Populations and time horizon. We'll capture the level of granularity per metric in Part 5 — this section is the strategic context around it.

questions.prompt: Which populations concern you most right now?
Recent shifts in the business worth flagging?
When you think about metrics, which do you need more — backwards reporting or forward-looking planning?

parts.title: Output consumers
parts.intro: Who's going to use these insights, and how often.

questions.prompt: Who needs to see these metrics?
Are there metrics that should be restricted to certain audiences?
questions.note_prompt: Quick note on what should be restricted?

questions.prompt: How often do you want updated insights?

parts.title: Metrics that matter
parts.intro: Two phases. First we'll prioritise the metric categories so we can focus interview time well. Then we'll walk through the metrics that earn the time.
parts.ranking_intro: Before we walk through metrics, let's prioritise. Drag the categories below into priority order — top is most important to you, bottom is least.
parts.ack_message: Got it. I'll focus the storytelling around your ranking — most time on what matters most. Ready to start?
parts.granularity_followup_prompt: Where would this matter most for you? Pick all that apply.
parts.nice_acknowledgment: Got it. We'll keep this one in the wider list but it won't drive data discovery.
parts.no_acknowledgment: Understood. We'll keep this out of scope and not chase the data for it.
parts.already_followup_prompt: Which system shows this to you today? Pick all that apply.

parts.title: Data availability
parts.intro: Specific data questions for the metrics you flagged Top tier or Second tier. I've made my best guess at which system holds each data point — flag if I've guessed wrong, if you'd rather we use one of your custom recons, or if you're not sure where it lives.
parts.intro_with_recons_template: Heads-up: you mentioned {{n}} custom report{{s}} — {{recon_list}}. For any question below where one of these is the better source than the system, just say so in the note and we'll route the data through it.
